body: Current main branch simulations fail with runaway eta values at high latitudes - e.g. (Longrun; 16 horizontal elements in atmos cubed-sphere mesh) This PR introduces an ocean flux high-lat mask which zeros all ocean surface flux updates where |lat| ≥ 78° (user defined polar mask latitude), so the ocean is not forced in that band. This fixes an issue with inconsistent flux balances in the overlap area and stabilizes simulations for up to 2 weeks.
title: Fix runaway eta (free sfc displacement) at high latitudes

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Fixes for the aquaplanet and subseasonal calibration in the [latest failing longruns](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-longruns/builds/1064). The other runs failed either because of artifact issues or stability problems.

1. Conservation error increased between Feb 15-22, I suspect due to the update to ClimaAtmos v0.35.4 in https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCoupler.jl/pull/1746. This PR increases the threshold in the test.
2. Typo in the pathname used by the subseasonal calibration test. Now it's parsed from the `CALIBRATE_CONFIG` object rather than hardcoding the same string in two places.

## Content
- [x] increase conservation check limit
- [x] get output path from config file in calibration


<!---
Review checklist

- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

title: longrun fixes

body: This PR adds functions for updating the start and end dates of a simulation and adding parameter toml files to the config dict.
title: Add utility functions to CalibrationTools

body:
This PR adds data loaders for other datasets in ClimaArtifacts. These datasets include
- `radiation_obs`
- `precipitation_obs`
- `era5_monthly_averages_pressure_levels_1979_2024`
- `modis_lwp_iwp`

This is needed for calibration to simplify the process of getting variables from the dataset in a standardized way.
title: Add other data loaders
